title: Crypt of Chaos - A Small-Box Dungeon Crawl
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ol2a1T0BgrM

Daniel (@dungeondive) takes a quick look at Crypt of Chaos (a review copy from Crystal Dagger Games), a small-box dungeon crawl he files alongside Bag of Dungeon and Dungeon Breakout — even simpler than those. You pick one of six heroes (each with a stat line and one special ability), then build the crypt tile by tile as you draw cards, fighting card-driven monsters that guard treasure, dodging traps, and hunting the lost sword and the key of freedom needed to confront the final dragon and escape.

He frames it honestly as "dungeon solitaire" — the equivalent of unwinding with a deck of cards in front of the TV. Combat uses a shuffled card deck rather than dice; healing is scarce, so you're perpetually near death (he hasn't won in three tries). He notes a visual disconnect — pulpy box and rulebook fonts clashing with realistic modern-fantasy character art — and his recurring small-box wish: one more layer. His concrete pitch: bolt on a Dark-Venture-style book of random events keyed to certain tiles, adding NPCs, puzzles and non-combat encounters — the "spice" these stripped-down crawls lack.

Easy to learn, travel-friendly, genuinely low-complexity — a fit for a particular relaxed mood, but no emergent narrative.

----

When you want pure "dungeon solitaire," does a game this simple hit the spot, or would you reach for a phone roguelike instead?
